UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile with administrative access to the ChemGuard AI dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: Unique username for login context and display.

role.description: User's role or permission level within the system (e.g., 'Admin', 'Operator', 'Viewer').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

ChemicalBatch.title: ChemicalBatch
ChemicalBatch.description: Represents a specific batch of a chemical, tracking its quantity, dates, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChemicalBatch entity.

batchNumber.description: Unique identifier assigned to this specific chemical batch.

chemicalInventoryId.description: Reference to the ChemicalInventory entry describing the type of chemical in this batch. (Relationship: ChemicalInventory 1:N ChemicalBatch)

quantity.description: Current quantity of the chemical in this batch.

unit.description: Unit of measurement for the chemical quantity (e.g., 'kg', 'L').

manufactureDate.description: Date when this chemical batch was manufactured.

expiryDate.description: Date when this chemical batch is no longer safe or effective for use.

storageLocation.description: Physical location where this chemical batch is currently stored.

status.description: Current status of the chemical batch (e.g., 'Available', 'In Use', 'Expired', 'Disposed').

hazardLevel.description: Categorization of the hazard risk associated with this batch (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this chemical batch record was created.

ChemicalInventory.title: ChemicalInventory
ChemicalInventory.description: Defines the properties of a chemical type, independent of specific batches.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChemicalInventory entity.

chemicalName.description: Common or official name of the chemical.

casNumber.description: Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) Registry Number, a unique numerical identifier for chemical substances.

hazardStatement.description: Summary of primary hazards associated with the chemical, often from GHS.

safetyDataSheetUrl.description: URL to the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for this chemical.

maxStorageCapacity.description: Maximum recommended storage quantity for this chemical type within a facility.

unit.description: Default unit of measurement for this chemical (e.g., 'kg', 'L').

regulatoryCodes.description: List of relevant regulatory codes or classifications (e.g., UN numbers, GHS pictograms).

description.description: Detailed description of the chemical and its typical uses.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this chemical inventory record was created.

ShipmentRequest.title: ShipmentRequest
ShipmentRequest.description: Manages requests and tracking information for chemical shipments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShipmentRequest entity.

shipmentIdentifier.description: Human-readable identifier for the shipment request.

requestorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the shipment request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ShipmentRequest)

chemicalBatchIds.description: References to the ChemicalBatch entities included in this shipment. (Relationship: ChemicalBatch N:N ShipmentRequest)

originLocation.description: Starting point of the shipment (e.g., 'Warehouse A', 'Facility XYZ').

expectedDepartureTime.description: Anticipated date and time for the shipment to depart.

expectedArrivalTime.description: Anticipated date and time for the shipment to arrive.

actualDepartureTime.description: Actual date and time when the shipment departed.

actualArrivalTime.description: Actual date and time when the shipment arrived.

status.description: Current status of the shipment (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Transit', 'Delivered', 'Canceled').

trackingNumber.description: External tracking number provided by the logistics carrier.

assignedVehicleId.description: Reference to the HardwareDevice (e.g., GPS tracker) assigned to monitor the vehicle for this shipment. (Relationship: HardwareDevice 1:N ShipmentRequest)

currentLatitude.description: Current latitude coordinate of the shipment for real-time tracking.

currentLongitude.description: Current longitude coordinate of the shipment for real-time tracking.

routeDataUrl.description: URL to a GeoJSON or other format file containing the planned or actual route data for map visualization.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the shipment request was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the shipment request.

HardwareDevice.title: HardwareDevice
HardwareDevice.description: Records information about various IoT devices used for monitoring chemical batches and shipments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HardwareDevice entity.

deviceIdentifier.description: Unique identifier for the hardware device (e.g., asset tag, serial).

deviceName.description: Friendly name of the device (e.g., 'Warehouse GPS Unit', 'Truck Temp Sensor A').

deviceType.description: Type of hardware device (e.g., 'GPS', 'Temperature Sensor', 'Tamper Sensor', 'Checkpoint Camera').

serialNumber.description: Unique serial number of the device.

status.description: Current operational status of the device (e.g., 'Online', 'Offline', 'Error', 'Maintenance').

lastReportedAt.description: Timestamp of the last data transmission or status update from the device.

firmwareVersion.description: Firmware version running on the device.

installationLocation.description: Physical location where the device is installed or last known fixed location.

associatedShipmentId.description: Reference to the ShipmentRequest currently associated with this device, if applicable. (Relationship: HardwareDevice N:1 ShipmentRequest)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the hardware device record was created.

UsageLog.title: UsageLog
UsageLog.description: Logs all movements, consumption, and changes to chemical batch quantities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UsageLog entity.

logIdentifier.description: Unique identifier for the specific usage log entry.

timestamp.description: Date and time when the usage event occurred.

chemicalBatchId.description: Reference to the ChemicalBatch that was affected by this usage event. (Relationship: ChemicalBatch 1:N UsageLog)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed or recorded this usage action. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UsageLog)

quantityChanged.description: Quantity of chemical affected by the action.

unit.description: Unit of measurement for the quantity changed (e.g., 'kg', 'L').

newQuantityInBatch.description: Resulting quantity in the associated chemical batch after this action.

location.description: Location where the usage action took place.

notes.description: Optional notes or comments about the usage event.

Alert.title: Alert
Alert.description: Stores information about critical events and anomalies detected by the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Alert entity.

alertIdentifier.description: Unique identifier for the specific alert instance.

timestamp.description: Date and time when the alert was triggered.

alertType.description: Categorization of the alert (e.g., 'Tampering Detected', 'Temperature Excursion', 'Off-Route Deviation', 'Low Stock', 'Compliance Breach').

status.description: Current status of the alert (e.g., 'New', 'Acknowledged', 'Resolved', 'False Positive').

location.description: Location or coordinates where the alert originated.

shipmentId.description: Reference to the ShipmentRequest related to this alert, if applicable. (Relationship: ShipmentRequest N:1 Alert)

hardwareDeviceId.description: Reference to the HardwareDevice that triggered this alert, if applicable. (Relationship: HardwareDevice N:1 Alert)

chemicalBatchId.description: Reference to the ChemicalBatch involved in this alert, if applicable. (Relationship: ChemicalBatch N:1 Alert)

description.description: Detailed message or explanation of the alert.

riskLevel.description: Overall risk level associated with this alert, often AI-determined (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

resolvedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who resolved the alert, if applicable. (Relationship: UserProfile N:1 Alert)

resolvedAt.description: Timestamp when the alert was marked as resolved.

ComplianceLog.title: ComplianceLog
ComplianceLog.description: Records compliance checks and their results against predefined rules and regulations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ComplianceLog entity.

logIdentifier.description: Unique identifier for the specific compliance log entry.

timestamp.description: Date and time when the compliance check was performed.

complianceRuleName.description: Name of the compliance rule or regulation being checked (e.g., 'Temperature_Range_Compliance', 'Storage_Limit_Compliance').

isCompliant.description: Indicates whether the entity was found to be compliant with the rule.

details.description: Detailed explanation of the compliance status, including any non-compliance findings.

relatedEntityId.description: ID of the entity (e.g., ShipmentRequest, ChemicalBatch, HardwareDevice) to which this compliance log entry pertains.

relatedEntityType.description: Type of the entity to which this compliance log entry pertains (e.g., 'ShipmentRequest', 'ChemicalBatch', 'HardwareDevice').

auditorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed or initiated the compliance audit, if applicable. (Relationship: UserProfile N:1 ComplianceLog)

complianceScoreChange.description: Change in the overall compliance score related to this event, for trend analysis.

currentComplianceScore.description: Overall compliance score at the time of this log entry.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is typically restricted to the owning user (matching `request.auth.uid`) or authorized administrators. The 'role' field is for application logic, not directly for Firestore security rules (see /roles_admin, /roles_operator, /roles_viewer for security rule roles).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, typically matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for administrative roles. A document's existence at this path signifies that the `userId` is an 'Admin'. Used for security rule authorization via existence checks, adhering to Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds the 'Admin' role.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for operator roles. A document's existence at this path signifies that the `userId` is an 'Operator'. Used for security rule authorization via existence checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds the 'Operator' role.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for viewer roles. A document's existence at this path signifies that the `userId` is a 'Viewer'. Used for security rule authorization via existence checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds the 'Viewer' role.

definition.description: Master list of chemical definitions. Access is typically role-based, allowing all authorized users (e.g., 'Viewer' role or higher) to read/list, and 'Admin'/'Operator' roles to manage.

params.name: chemicalInventoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chemical type in the inventory.

definition.description: Records specific batches of chemicals. Access is role-based; 'Viewer' roles can read/list, 'Admin'/'Operator' roles can manage. `chemicalInventoryId` is a reference, not an authorization dependency.

params.name: chemicalBatchId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chemical batch.

definition.description: Manages chemical shipment requests. Access is role-based (Admin/Operator can manage all), and also allows requestors to access their own requests. The 'requestorId' is denormalized within the document for Authorization Independence.

params.name: shipmentRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific shipment request.

definition.description: Stores information about IoT hardware devices. Access is role-based; 'Viewer' roles can read/list, 'Admin'/'Operator' roles can manage. `associatedShipmentId` is a reference, not an authorization dependency.

params.name: hardwareDeviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific hardware device.

definition.description: Audit logs for chemical usage. Access is primarily role-based for reading/listing by 'Viewer' or higher roles. `userId` and `chemicalBatchId` are references, not authorization dependencies.

params.name: usageLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific usage log entry.

definition.description: Stores critical system alerts. Access is role-based; 'Viewer' roles can read/list, 'Admin'/'Operator' roles can update status. Related IDs are references, not authorization dependencies.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific alert.

definition.description: Records compliance check results. Access is role-based for reading/listing by 'Viewer' or higher roles. `auditorId` and `relatedEntityId` are references, not authorization dependencies.

params.name: complianceLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific compliance log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for ChemGuard AI Dashboard prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy revolves around **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** and **Structural Segregation** to simplify security rules and enable robust Query-Acceptance Patterns (QAPs).

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) Explanation:**
1.  **Role-Based Access Control (DBAC):** Instead of embedding roles directly into the `/users/{userId}` document and then performing a `get()` call within rules to retrieve the user's role, we employ the 'Existence over Content' strategy. Dedicated, top-level collections are used for role assignment (e.g., `/roles_admin/{userId}`, `/roles_operator/{userId}`, `/roles_viewer/{userId}`). A user's role is determined by the *existence* of a document with their `uid` in the respective role collection. This allows security rules to check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` without creating a hierarchical dependency on the `UserProfile` data itself. This completely avoids `get()` calls on application-specific data for general role authorization, fulfilling the critical mandate.
2.  **Document-Level Ownership/Context Denormalization:** For entities where individual user ownership or context is crucial for access (e.g., a `ShipmentRequest` initiated by a specific user), the relevant `ownerId` or `requestorId` is denormalized directly into the document itself (e.g., `requestorId` in `ShipmentRequest`). This allows security rules to evaluate ownership simply by comparing `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.requestorId`, again, without requiring `get()` calls on other documents like the user's profile.

**Query-Acceptance Patterns (QAPs) Explanation:**
1.  **Homogeneous Security Posture (Structural Segregation):** Most core application collections (`chemical_inventory`, `chemical_batches`, `hardware_devices`, `alerts`, `compliance_logs`, `usage_logs`) are designed with a homogeneous security posture. This means all documents within these collections share the same authorization rules (e.g., all `ChemicalInventory` documents are readable by 'Viewer' role and writeable by 'Admin'/'Operator' roles). This enables secure `list` operations because if a user has read access to the collection, they can securely query and receive all documents that satisfy the rule without the rule acting as a filter. The client simply lists the collection, and the rule validates the request based on the user's role.
2.  **User-Specific Item Listing (e.g., ShipmentRequest):** For collections like `/shipment_requests`, where a user might only see their own initiated requests, the security rules will enforce that client-side queries from non-admin/non-operator users *must* include a `where('requestorId', '==', request.auth.uid)` filter. This ensures that the client actively specifies the data they are authorized to access, and the rule validates this intent, adhering to the 'rules are not filters' principle. Admin/Operator roles, having broader access, would not be subject to this filter requirement by the rules.

Overall, this structure uses clear, path-based access for user-specific data, dedicated collections for role management, and denormalization of critical authorization fields to eliminate problematic `get()` calls in security rules, fostering a robust, debuggable, and scalable Firestore backend for ChemGuard AI.
